meta.name: JSS-9 Job Satisfaction Survey Brief
meta.fullName: Job Satisfaction Survey - 9 Item Brief Version
meta.description: The JSS-9 is a brief version of the Job Satisfaction Survey that measures overall job satisfaction across key facets including pay, promotion, supervision, benefits, recognition, procedures, coworkers, meaningfulness, and communication.
meta.author: Spector PE (1985, adapted)
meta.reference: Spector PE. Measurement of human service staff satisfaction: Development of the Job Satisfaction Survey. Am J Community Psychol. 1985;13(6):693-713.
meta.instruction: Please indicate your level of agreement with each statement about your current job.

questions.text: I feel I am being paid a fair amount for the work I do
There is really too little chance for promotion on my job
My supervisor is quite competent in doing his/her job
I am not satisfied with the benefits I receive
When I do a good job, I receive the recognition for it that I should receive
Many of our rules and procedures make doing a good job difficult
I like the people I work with
I sometimes feel my job is meaningless
Communications seem good within this organization

ranges.label: Very Dissatisfied
ranges.description: Your score indicates strong dissatisfaction with your job across multiple areas. This level of dissatisfaction may significantly impact your well-being and performance.
suggestions: Identify the specific aspects of your job causing the most dissatisfaction
Consider discussing concerns with your supervisor or HR department
Explore whether a role change or job transition might be beneficial
Seek support from a career counselor
Take care of your mental health, as job dissatisfaction can affect overall well-being

ranges.label: Dissatisfied
ranges.description: Your score suggests moderate job dissatisfaction. Some aspects of your work are not meeting your expectations.
suggestions: Identify which specific facets (pay, recognition, supervision) need the most attention
Have an open conversation with your supervisor about your concerns
Explore training or development opportunities that could improve your situation
Build stronger connections with supportive coworkers

ranges.label: Ambivalent
ranges.description: Your score reflects mixed feelings about your job. You may be satisfied with some aspects and dissatisfied with others.
suggestions: Identify which aspects you find most and least satisfying
Focus on building on the positive elements of your job
Address areas of concern proactively with your employer
Consider professional development to expand your options

ranges.label: Satisfied
ranges.description: Your score indicates overall satisfaction with your job. You feel positively about most aspects of your work environment.
suggestions: Continue engaging in activities that contribute to your job satisfaction
Share your positive experiences to help create a better workplace for others
Stay aware of potential burnout even when satisfied
Continue seeking growth and development opportunities
